steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The blank comes after “book packaging” and before the verb “specializes”. We need a noun to fill the blank and a connector to link it to the verb.

steps.title: Apply the relative clause rule
steps.content:
The noun “company” fills the blank, and the verb “specializes” requires a relative pronoun to introduce it.

The rule is that “that” introduces relative clauses without a preceding comma. “Company that specializes in...” correctly applies this rule. Omitting the pronoun leaves an ungrammatical gap, and placing a comma before “that” incorrectly treats the identifying clause as nonessential.

That is the correct answer.
